# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM debian:bookworm-slim

# Create a non-root user for running the application
RUN groupadd --gid 1001 appgroup && \
    useradd --uid 1001 --gid appgroup --shell /bin/bash --create-home appuser

# Set environment variables to prevent interactive prompts and reduce image noise
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Update package lists and upgrade existing packages for security patches
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install essential system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    gnupg && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js from the official NodeSource repository using pinned major version
RUN mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings && \
    curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/gpgkey/nodesource-repo.gpg.key | \
    gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg && \
    echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg] https://deb.nodesource.com/node_20.x nodistro main" | \
    tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/nodesource.list && \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install build tools required for native Node.js modules
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    make \
    g++ && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install additional system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    openssh-client \
    wget \
    dnsutils \
    net-tools \
    procps \
    jq && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install tini as a proper init process to handle signals correctly
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends tini && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set up application directory with proper ownership
RUN mkdir -p /app && chown appuser:appgroup /app

WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files first for better Docker layer caching
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup package.json package-lock.json* ./

# Switch to non-root user before installing dependencies
USER appuser

# Install Node.js dependencies
# Using --ignore-scripts initially for security, then running prepare explicitly if needed
RUN npm ci --ignore-scripts && \
    npm cache clean --force

# Copy application source code with proper ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . .

# Ensure no secrets or sensitive files are included (defense in depth)
# .dockerignore should handle this, but verify critical paths don't exist
RUN test ! -f .env || (echo "ERROR: .env file should not be in the image" && exit 1) && \
    test ! -f .env.local || (echo "ERROR: .env.local file should not be in the image" && exit 1) && \
    test ! -d .git || (echo "ERROR: .git directory should not be in the image" && exit 1)

# Expose application port (non-privileged port)
EXPOSE 3000

# Set secure Node.js runtime options
ENV NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=512 --disallow-code-generation-from-strings"

# Health check to ensure the application is responsive
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=15s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

# Use tini as entrypoint for proper signal handling and zombie process reaping
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]

# Start the application
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
